title: lint_off does not suppress all EOFNEWLINE warnings
body:
Using Verilator 4.214 2021-10-17 rev v4.214
(no, I've not tried master, but that's the latest tag as of this morning)

Consider the following test.sv:

```
module test (
  input   wire      clk,

  input   wire [4:0] a,
  input   wire [3:0] b,

  output reg  [3:0] y
);

always @(posedge clk)
  y <= #1 a + b;

endmodule
```

without a newline at the end of the file.

`verilator -Wall --lint-only test.sv` correctly identifies 3 issues:

```
%Warning-EOFNEWLINE: test.sv:13:11: Missing newline at end of file (POSIX 3.206).
%Warning-ASSIGNDLY: test.sv:11:9: Unsupported: Ignoring delay on this assignment/primitive.
%Warning-WIDTH: test.sv:11:5: Operator ASSIGNDLY expects 4 bits on the Assign RHS, but Assign RHS's ADD generates 5 bits.
```

I then create a waivers.vlt to excuse my sloppy coding:

```
`verilator_config

lint_off -rule EOFNEWLINE -file "*test.sv"
lint_off -rule ASSIGNDLY -file "*test.sv"
lint_off -rule WIDTH -file "*test.sv"
```

and go again with `verilator -Wall --lint-only waivers.vlt test.sv`

```
%Warning-EOFNEWLINE: test.sv:13:11: Missing newline at end of file (POSIX 3.206).
```

but I expected a clean result.

~~In this case the ASSIGNDLY warning has gone, but in a larger codebase they get through (I've no idea if it's all of them).~~
